title: centaurea phrygia subsp. moesiaca
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. moesiaca, also known as the Balkan knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and enhancing the immune system. Centaurea phrygia subsp. moesiaca can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and capsules, and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
